channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Government Deploys Child Protection and Counselling Teams After Deadly Utumishi Girls Academy Fire
item.description: The Kenyan government has appealed to the public and media houses to respect the privacy and dignity of students and families affected by the tragic dormitory fire at Utumishi Girls Academy Senior Sch...

item.title: Matthew Perry's Personal Assistant Sentenced to 41 Months in Prison for Role in Ketamine Overdose Death
item.description: Kenneth Iwamasa, the live-in personal assistant to the late 'Friends' actor Matthew Perry, was sentenced to three years and five months (41 months) in federal prison for his central role in the ketami...

item.title: Scrutiny intensifies over NACC leadership and Defence-linked investigations following Paul Brereton’s resignation
item.description: The inaugural commissioner of Australia’s National Anti-Corruption Commission (NACC), Paul Brereton, has unexpectedly resigned, prompting renewed scrutiny of the agency’s performance and internal cult...

item.title: Nicky Chandam’s photographic documentation of Manipuri performance arts amid conflict
item.description: The article explores the photographic practice and ongoing exhibition of Manipur-based photographer Nicky Chandam, whose work documents live performance arts across India, particularly Manipuri cultur...

item.title: Ottawa portfolio manager reflects on career lessons and the value of humility in financial advice
item.description: Benoît Poliquin, president and portfolio manager at Ottawa-based Exponent Investment Management Inc., shares personal and professional reflections on his journey in the financial services industry. Hi...

item.title: An analysis of Iran’s three-tier governance model and its approach to state resilience
item.description: The article examines Iran’s governance system, arguing that its political structure is deliberately designed to preserve sovereignty and withstand sustained external pressure, particularly from Wester...

item.title: Opinion piece argues that U.S. democracy is heavily influenced by money and lobbying power
item.description: The article is an opinion commentary that questions the integrity of democratic processes in the United States, arguing that political outcomes are heavily shaped by wealthy donors, lobbying groups, a...

item.title: Pelé recognised as the greatest World Cup player after historic three-tournament triumph
item.description: This article from The Independent argues that Pelé stands as the greatest player in World Cup history, surpassing other legends such as Diego Maradona and Lionel Messi. The central justification is hi...

item.title: Ron Baron Bets Heavily on Elon Musk’s Companies as SpaceX Nears a Possible IPO
item.description: The article focuses on Ron Baron, the 83-year-old celebrity investor whose firm has made a major bet on Elon Musk’s companies, especially SpaceX and Tesla. Baron’s largest fund has had a weak year, bu...

item.title: Broker choice becomes critical as USD/ZAR volatility rises during SARB rate uncertainty
item.description: South Africans trading the USD/ZAR currency pair are currently navigating a highly volatile environment shaped by both local and global economic forces. While domestic indicators such as inflation and...

item.title: Canadiens remain confident ahead of crucial Game 4 against Hurricanes
item.description: The Montreal Canadiens are pushing back against the idea that their playoff hopes are finished as they prepare for Game 4 against the Carolina Hurricanes. Despite facing a difficult position in their ...

item.title: Nicolas Cage says Breaking Bad changed his perspective on television roles
item.description: Nicolas Cage has revealed that the acclaimed AMC series Breaking Bad significantly altered his outlook on working in television. In an interview with Variety, Cage explained that during the Covid-19 p...

item.title: Andrew Durham and Sofia Coppola discuss Fairyland, a memoir-inspired film about queer parenting and the AIDS era
item.description: Andrew Durham and Sofia Coppola have discussed their collaboration on Fairyland, a film adapted from Alysia Abbott’s memoir Fairyland: A Memoir of My Father. The story follows a young girl growing up ...

item.title: Fact-check finds viral Macron quote about Kenyans was fabricated
item.description: A viral graphic circulating on Facebook in Kenya has been found to falsely attribute offensive remarks to French president Emmanuel Macron. The graphic claimed that Macron described Kenyans as “ill ma...

item.title: PNP forensic team recovers about 2,000 bone fragments from Taal Lake in missing sabungeros investigation
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) Forensic Group has reported the recovery of approximately 2,000 bone fragments from Taal Lake as part of the ongoing investigation into the disappearance of severa...

item.title: PM Shehbaz Sharif exchanges Eid ul Adha greetings with military leadership and political figures
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif on Wednesday extended Eid ul Adha greetings to Pakistan’s top military leadership, including the Chief of Army Staff and Chief of Defence Forces Field Marshal Syed Asim M...

item.title: Jakub Mensik struggles physically after five-set victory at Roland-Garros
item.description: Jakub Mensik has been left in significant physical discomfort following a demanding five-set victory at Roland-Garros. The young tennis player battled through a gruelling match that tested his enduran...

item.title: Wild meat in Central Africa: balancing conservation and food security
item.description: This report explains the important role wild meat plays in Central Africa, especially for millions of people living around the Congo rainforest. In many rural areas, domestic livestock production is l...

item.title: First Afghan Woman Successfully Climbs Mount Everest
item.description: An Afghan woman has achieved a historic milestone by becoming the first woman from Afghanistan to successfully climb Mount Everest, the world’s highest peak. The achievement was reported by CNN, with ...

item.title: New Jersey proposes rules requiring data centers to cover their own energy and infrastructure costs
item.description: New Jersey Governor Mikie Sherrill has unveiled a new policy proposal aimed at changing how data centers operate in the state, particularly in relation to their energy consumption and impact on public...
